name: Measures, Instruments, Derived Scores, Variables & Availability Summaries
note: Combined tier: former Tier 5 (Measures & Instruments) and Tier 6 (Derived Scores, Variables & Availability Summaries) merged into a single Tier 5. Shared anchor fields were de-duplicated; each field's original tier is recorded in 'source_tier'.
purpose: Describe what was measured (constructs, instrument identity, administration modality/technology) at a level that supports discovery and basic interpretability. Make derived outputs interpretable (what a score/variable represents, and its general derivation form), especially when item/trial-level data aren’t shared. Help users answer “is there enough data for my question?” without accessing participant-level files, counts, ranges, units, or timepoint-scoped summaries.
effort: Medium - High (depends on how well instruments are documented and whether devices/platform info is centralized) / High (often requires analyst input or codebook review, requires computing/curating summaries; may be restricted depending on policy→ may keep optional/add to study later, i.e., "enrichment".

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value applies to an individual participant entity (participant-level).
The metadata value applies to a cohort or group of participants considered as a unit.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

valid_values.definition: The metadata value represents an individual-level value (not aggregated).
The metadata value represents an aggregate summary across a defined population scope.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

valid_values.definition: The metadata value summarizes or pertains to the full study population.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a specific study arm or group.
The metadata value summarizes or pertains to a defined cohort within the study.

fields.name: deviceType
fields.description: The type of digital device used for data collection, applicable only when data are collected digitally.

valid_values.definition: Data were collected using a smartphone device.
Data were collected using a tablet device (e.g., iPad, Android tablet).
Data were collected using a wearable device (e.g., smartwatch, activity tracker).
Data were collected using a desktop or laptop computer.
Data were collected using a purpose-built or study-issued research device not captured by other categories.
Data were collected using more than one digital device type (e.g., both smartphone and tablet).
Digital device type is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: softwarePlatform
fields.description: The software environment or application used to deliver, collect, or manage digitally collected data.

valid_values.definition: Data were delivered, collected, or managed using REDCap.
Data were delivered or collected using a study-specific custom-built application.
Data were delivered or collected using a commercial software platform (non-custom).
Data were delivered or collected using an open-source software platform or framework.
Data were delivered or collected through a browser-based web application.
More than one software platform was used for digital data collection.
Software platform is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: inputMethod
fields.description: The primary method by which participant input or data capture occurs during digital data collection.

valid_values.definition: Data capture occurred primarily via touchscreen interaction.
Data capture occurred primarily via typed keyboard input.
Data capture occurred primarily via mouse or trackpad input.
Data capture occurred primarily via sensors (e.g., accelerometer, GPS, heart rate).
Data capture occurred primarily via voice input or audio capture.
More than one input method was used for data capture.
Input method is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: administrationMode
fields.description: The manner in which a digital assessment or data collection activity is administered to participants.

valid_values.definition: Participants completed the activity independently without live administration.
The activity was administered by an interviewer (e.g., structured interview).
The activity was administered by a clinician or trained assessor in a clinical role.
Participants completed the activity with assistance (e.g., help operating device or understanding prompts).
The study used more than one administration mode across participants or timepoints.
Administration mode is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: manufacturerModel
fields.description: Device make/model. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: operatingSystem
fields.description: OS and version. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: appPlatform
fields.description: App or platform used. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: sensors
fields.description: Sensors enabled (GPS, accelerometer, etc.). Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: measureID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

measureID
fields.description: Persistent per-measure row identifier. (ex., "MEAS_SCA_STROOP_v1")

fields.name: measuredConstruct
fields.description: A conceptual domain or construct that a study collected data on (e.g., depression, working memory, physical activity).

valid_values.definition: Construct related to worry, fear, or anxious arousal.
Construct related to perceived or physiological stress responses.
Construct related to emotional state, mood, or affective tone.
Construct related to monitoring and modulating emotional responses.
Construct related to positive mental health, life satisfaction, or well-being.
Construct related to social behavior, relationships, or social role performance.
Construct related to perceived quality of life, functioning, or life impact.
Construct related to behavioral or neurobehavioral symptom expression.
Construct related to orienting, sustained attention, or selective attention.
Construct related to holding and manipulating information over short periods.
Construct related to planning, control, and goal-directed regulation of behavior.
Construct related to switching, adapting, or shifting between tasks or mental sets.
Construct related to suppressing or inhibiting automatic or prepotent responses.
Construct related to the speed of cognitive processing or response.
Construct related to acquisition of knowledge or skills over time.
Construct related to encoding, storage, and retrieval of information.
Construct related to comprehension or production of spoken or written language.
Construct related to spatial perception, visual construction, or visuospatial reasoning.
Construct related to evaluating options and selecting actions under uncertainty or choice.
Construct related to movement, motor performance, or motor control.
Construct related to postural stability, balance, or walking gait.
Construct related to sensory perception, integration, or sensory responsiveness.
Construct related to sleep patterns, sleep quality, or sleep disturbance.
Construct related to tiredness, exhaustion, or fatigue-related impairment.
Construct related to pain experience, severity, or pain-related interference.
Construct related to cognitive decline or impaired cognitive functioning.
Construct related to bodily movement, activity level, or exercise behavior.
Construct related to time spent inactive or in low-energy postures.
Construct related to nutrition, diet patterns, or food consumption.
Construct related to body size or composition measures (e.g., BMI, adiposity).
Construct related to cardiovascular/metabolic status (e.g., blood pressure, glucose risk).
Construct related to breathing, lung function, or respiratory symptoms.
Construct related to overall health, self-rated health, or general functioning.
Construct related to attainment of developmental milestones or developmental progress.
Construct related to adaptive behaviors and everyday functioning.
Construct related to skills for independent living and daily activities.
Construct related to education level, schooling, or educational outcomes.
Construct related to work participation, employment, or occupational performance.
Construct related to environmental exposures or external contextual factors.
Construct related to socioeconomic conditions (e.g., income, education, neighborhood factors).
Construct related to significant life events or stressor exposures.
Construct related to seasonal patterns or time-of-year influences.
Construct related to demographic characteristics (e.g., age, sex, race/ethnicity, education).
Construct related to medical/clinical history or prior diagnoses/treatments.
Construct related to medication exposure, use patterns, or medication history.
Construct related to behaviors affecting health (e.g., smoking, alcohol use, diet, exercise).
Construct not captured by the listed categories.
Construct is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: constructDescription
fields.description: One-sentence description of how the construct was operationalized in the study.

fields.name: measureType
fields.description: The general type of measure used to assess a construct.

valid_values.definition: A self-report or informant-report instrument collected via written or electronic questions (including scales and inventories).
A performance-based task designed to assess cognitive processes (e.g., reaction time, accuracy, task switching).
A clinician- or assessor-administered evaluation or standardized clinical test/battery.
A measure based on observed behavior recorded or rated in a structured manner.
A measure derived from passive or active sensor signals (e.g., accelerometry, GPS, heart rate).
A structured or semi-structured interview administered by an interviewer to collect responses or ratings.
A measure combining multiple variables or sources into a single index value (not tied to a single instrument).
A measure type not captured by the listed categories.

fields.name: isValidatedMeasure
fields.description: Indicates whether a measure or instrument used to assess a construct has been previously validated.

valid_values.definition: The measure or instrument has been previously validated.
The measure or instrument has not been validated.
Some aspects of the measure have been validated; full validation is incomplete.
Validation status is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: measureDescription
fields.description: A brief description of how a measure was administered and what it was designed to assess.

fields.name: instrumentID
fields.header_note:
if not provided as a row identifier

instrumentID
fields.description: a stable identifier used to reference a specific instrument, measure, or assessment used in a study (e.g., a validated questionnaire, cognitive task, interview protocol, or sensor-based assessment).

fields.name: instrumentName
fields.description: Name of the instrument or scale used (e.g., PHQ-9, NIH Toolbox).

fields.name: instrumentSource
fields.description: Where the instrument originated

fields.name: instrumentCitation
fields.description: The primary citation(s) describing the instrument/task implementation used in the study (e.g., published paper, manual, DOI).

fields.name: instrumentVersion
fields.description: Version identifier for the instrument implementation used (e.g., vendor version, protocol revision, “v2”, “2016 revision”).

fields.name: stimulusResponseMode
fields.description: How participants interacted with the instrument during administration.

valid_values.definition: Participants responded using a physical or on-screen keyboard.
Participants responded using touchscreen input (e.g., tapping, swiping).
Participants responded using a mouse or trackpad pointing device.
Participants responded using spoken responses or voice input.
Responses were elicited and recorded by a clinician or trained assessor.
Participants responded using paper forms and writing instruments.
Response mode not captured by the listed categories.
Response mode is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: administrationInstructions
fields.description: The instructions provided to participants describing exactly how to perform the instrument/task (verbatim or summarized).

fields.name: administrationProcedure
fields.description: The source of the implementation (e.g., NIH Toolbox, PEBL, PsychoPy script, custom app).

valid_values.definition: Implementation follows a published manual, protocol, or standardized administration guide.
Implementation uses an NIH Toolbox instrument or platform.
Implementation uses the Psychology Experiment Building Language (PEBL) platform.
Implementation uses a PsychoPy script or PsychoPy-based application.
Implementation uses the E-Prime software platform.
Implementation is delivered/managed through REDCap.
Implementation is delivered/managed through Qualtrics.
Implementation is study-specific or custom-built (e.g., custom app or bespoke task code).
Implementation source is not captured by the listed categories.
Implementation source is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: protocolDescription
fields.description: The synID of the wiki that contains the protocol, study design and goals

fields.name: protocolURL
fields.description: Link to the protocol, manual, script, or documentation describing the instrument implementation.

fields.name: derivedVariableName
fields.description: The name of a derived variable or score as it appears in the dataset or metadata record.

fields.name: derivedVariableType
fields.description: A controlled category describing the general method or type of derivation used to compute a derived variable or score (e.g., sum score, composite score, standardized score).

valid_values.definition: Direct output from a measure without aggregation or transformation.
Derived variable computed as the sum of item, trial, or component values.
Derived variable computed as the arithmetic mean (average) of values.
Derived variable combining multiple items, trials, subscales, or measures into a single value.
Derived variable representing a defined subscale or domain within an instrument.
Derived variable transformed to a standardized metric (e.g., z-score, T-score, percentile) relative to a reference distribution.
Derived variable representing change between timepoints (e.g., follow-up minus baseline).
Derived variable summarizing longitudinal change over time (e.g., slope or growth parameter).
Derived variable indicating whether a value meets a defined cutoff or screening threshold (e.g., positive/negative).
Derived variable categorizing severity levels based on a score (e.g., mild/moderate/severe).
Derived variable estimating risk level using a rule-based or model-based approach.
Derived variable assigned by a clinician or trained assessor as a summary rating.
Derived variable summarizing adherence or completion (e.g., percent prompts completed).
Derived variable summarizing missing data patterns (e.g., proportion missing).
Derived variable summarizing timing of responses (e.g., latency, completion duration).
Derived variable computed by aggregating sensor data (e.g., daily mean steps, activity counts).
Derived variable capturing contextual patterns (e.g., mobility radius, time at home) derived from data streams.
Derived variable type not captured by the listed categories.
Derived variable type is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: variableSummary
fields.description: Aggregate summary statistics describing the observed distribution and availability of a variable in a dataset, without exposing participant-level values.

fields.name: scoreName
fields.description: Is the human-readable name of a score derived from a measure, as used or labeled by the study.

fields.name: scoreType
fields.description: Describes the general method or form by which a score was derived from a measure or assessment.

valid_values.definition: Direct score output from a measure without aggregation or transformation.
Score calculated as the sum of item, trial, or component values.
Score calculated as the arithmetic mean of item, trial, or component values.
Score combining multiple items, trials, subscales, or measures into a single value.
Score transformed to a standardized metric relative to a reference distribution (e.g., z, T, percentile).
Score produced via a defined algorithm or computational model beyond simple aggregation.
Score assigned by a clinician or trained assessor based on observation or a scoring rubric.
Higher-level index derived from one or more intermediate scores or metrics.

fields.name: derivedScore
fields.description: A summary description of a score derived from a measure, including how it was calculated.

fields.name: derivationMethod
fields.description: A high-level description of how a score was derived from raw or intermediate data.

valid_values.definition: Score is computed by summing item, trial, or component values into a total.
Score is computed as an arithmetic mean (average) of item, trial, or component values.
Score is computed by combining multiple items, trials, subscales, or measures into a single value.
Score is transformed to a standardized metric (e.g., z-score, T-score, percentile) relative to a reference distribution.
Score is produced using a defined algorithm, model, or computational procedure beyond simple aggregation.
Score is assigned by a clinician or trained assessor based on observation, judgment, or a scoring rubric.
Score derivation method is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: valueRange
fields.description: The observed minimum and maximum values of a continuous variable within the reported population scope.

fields.name: totalParticipants
fields.description: The total number of unique participants for whom data are available within the specified study, cohort, or scope.

fields.name: proportion
fields.description: A numeric value representing the fraction of a population associated with a given category, typically expressed as a percentage or decimal.

fields.name: dataAvailabilitySummary
fields.description: An aggregate description of the amount and demographic composition of available data for a study, cohort, or variable, provided without individual-level records.

fields.name: valueRangeMinValue
fields.description: The minimum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

fields.name: valueRangeMaxValue
fields.description: The maximum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

fields.name: unit
fields.description: The unit of measurement associated with a variable’s values or summary statistics (e.g., years, kg/m²).

valid_values.definition: Values are measured in years.
Values are measured in months.
Values are measured in days.
Values are measured in hours.
Values are measured in minutes.
Values are measured in seconds.
Values are expressed as a percentage (0–100).
Values represent an instrument- or study-defined score without a physical unit.
Values are measured in milliseconds.
Values are measured in kilograms.
Values are measured in centimeters.
Values are measured in meters.
Values are measured as kilograms per square meter (e.g., BMI).
Values are measured in millimeters of mercury (e.g., blood pressure).
Values are measured in beats per minute (e.g., heart rate).
Unit of measurement is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: timepoint
fields.description: A label identifying the study timepoint or phase at which the variable value or summary applies (e.g., baseline, follow-up).

valid_values.definition: Measurement pertains to the baseline timepoint (initial assessment reference point).
Measurement pertains to the screening phase prior to enrollment.
Measurement pertains to the enrollment timepoint (study entry).
Measurement pertains to the first scheduled study visit.
Measurement pertains to the second scheduled study visit.
Measurement pertains to a follow-up assessment (time unspecified).
Measurement pertains to the first follow-up assessment.
Measurement pertains to the second follow-up assessment.
Measurement pertains to the end-of-study assessment or final timepoint.
Timepoint is not specified or not tied to a discrete phase/visit.
Measurement summary aggregates across multiple timepoints.
Timepoint is not known or not recorded.

fields.name: rawOrDerived
fields.description: Raw vs derived data flag

fields.name: preprocessingSteps
fields.description: Preprocessing/cleaning applied
